@SoC_trilogy Playing around with different ways to ask it to spatialize how these concepts are related (davinci-003, temp=0) https://t.co/nLZyWT7Rmk
@SoC_trilogy This is all text-davinci-003 at temp=0
@SoC_trilogy 3 strange glitch token spatializations and one normal bonus control spatialization https://t.co/bZQA01lKkR

@SoC_trilogy A door to a room with a locked door, eh

You can also take all of those values in my compressed essence and ask the LLM to reverse them, and then project a person out of that; fleshing out a sort of evil shadow twin (Le Guin advised against this sort of work) https://t.co/u4RKUIP3JT

Given my derived magic words, I took all the context away, and temp=0 prompted the LLM to try and get a person back out of those words- and the results are surprisingly accurate and lossless... these magic words drive the robot towards the embedding space where my values live https://t.co/nO1JyFYPCo

Just realized I got distracted from my original goal: to find a sequence of tokens that will guide the LLM to the embedding space where... my essence lives, I guess. So I asked it to take all of those biographies and summarize (compress) them into tokens... https://t.co/ghTzq1wTyc
this is a lot of fun https://t.co/BePcs0YrJs https://t.co/MgJvjupzH6
someone send me chicago mayor election result data
I guess if we were really trying to get good ideas out of the LLM, we would have both a drunk agent and a sober agent, and deploy them at various times in the process like that Hemingway quote
in my caveman approach to GPT-3, temp usually approximates drunken/stonedness, if you turn the temp and frequency penalties up it just starts mashing on the keyboard like a drunkard
humans slowly realizing you have to let the robot get a little stoned to let it be creative and start to make novel connections (temp 0.9+ gang) https://t.co/i08B6fpltj
updated my calendly and will no longer be booking meetings on Fridays for the forseeable future

Finally, now that I had a bunch of differently-toned prompts describing my character, I could insert some other fictional characters in the world to interact with. I chose Sherlock Holmes. https://t.co/1GExll2M3j
Then, just for fun, I asked it to take the tone of a drunk lunatic at a bar who is a big fan of mine. Gold. https://t.co/vr3PKsgDYS
Next I asked the robot to write the bio in a poetic style reminiscent of Hunter S Thompson- which adds some charm to the writing style- I like these results a lot better https://t.co/QuzQltf7l5

I answered the additional questions and then had it generate a new bio, this time in the tone of an FBI Psychological Profile... which is interesting but not really what I am looking for https://t.co/2rXjq7BijV

I kept all of this in the prompt, and then I asked the robot if there was any additional information I was missing- then I had it ask me 5 more questions, it came up with:

"What are some of your earliest memories?"
"Who or what has inspired you to pursue a career in journalism?"

I wanted it to sound more like an academic report so I prompted "Summarize this biography and use more detailed words and take a more academic authoritative tone. Use extremely long academic words. Make it seem almost anthropological or scientific." https://t.co/tT8UPdHeZo

I then prompted it to use that information to generate a bio- which worked pretty well, if not a little boring. So we keep all of this, the questions, answers, and first draft- and start prompt tweaking to get it to work through different tones that might be interesting https://t.co/87N3gCAodA

The Robot generated 10 questions, which I then answered truthfully to the best of my ability... 

"What is your full name?"
"Where were you born and raised?"
"What are some of your hobbies?"
"What is something you have accomplished that you are proud of?"

got kind of obsessed last night with trying to get the AI to generate a deep and thoughtful bio for me- I started the prompt "The following 10 questions will allow me to write an extremely detailed description of you as a person..."
getting the robot to describe me through fictional character's eyes is the most fun I've had in ages https://t.co/PSjrrcykhc
